zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas of Brotherton 1st Earl of Norfolk於1300年6月1日出生在英國越可爾布洛瑟頓時，他的父親Edward I King of England60歲，母親Marguerite Capet de France Queen of England20歲。他於大約1318年與Alice de Hales結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1338年8月4日在英國素福爾可福拉苗林加姆 卡素享年38歲，埋葬在英國。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas of Brotherton 1st Earl of Norfolk于1300年6月1日出生在英国越可爾布洛瑟頓时，他的父亲Edward I King of England60岁，母亲Marguerite Capet de France Queen of England20岁。他于大约1318年与Alice de Hales结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1338年8月4日在英国素福爾可福拉苗林加姆 卡素享年38岁，埋葬在英国。

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Brotherton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Brotherton in North Yorkshire and occasionally from the now lost Brotherton in Hacheston, Suffolk; both are named with Old English brōthor ‘brother’ or the Old Norse personal name Bróthir + Old English tūn ‘farmstead, enclosure’.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas of Brotherton 1st Earl of Norfolk was born on 1 June 1300, in Brotherton, Yorkshire, England, his father, Edward I King of England, was 60 and his mother, Marguerite Capet de France Queen of England, was 20. He married Alice de Hales about 1318. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He died on 4 August 1338, in Framlingham Castle, Suffolk, England, at the age of 38, and was buried in England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas of Brotherton 1st Earl of Norfolk est né le 1 juin 1300, à Brotherton, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Edward I King of England, avait 60 ans et sa mère, Marguerite Capet de France Queen of England, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Alice de Hales environ 1318. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 4 août 1338, à Framlingham Castle, Suffolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 38 ans, et a été enterré à Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Thomas of Brotherton 1st Earl of Norfolk am 1. Juni 1300 in Brotherton, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Edward I King of England 60 und seine Mutter Marguerite Capet de France Queen of England 20. Er hat um 1318 Alice de Hales geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 4. August 1338 im Alter von 38 Jahren in Framlingham Castle, Suffolk, England und wurde in England begraben.

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas of Brotherton 1st Earl of Norfolk(이)가 1300년 6월 1일 영국 요르크실르 브로더턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Edward I King of England(은)는 60세였고, 그의 어머니 Marguerite Capet de France Queen of England(은)는 20세였다. 그는 대략 1318년 Alice de Hales(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1338년 8월 4일 영국 수폴크 프램링함 카스틀에서 38세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas of Brotherton 1st Earl of Norfolk nasceu em 1 de junho de 1300, em Brotherton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Edward I King of England, tinha 60 anos e sua mãe, Marguerite Capet de France Queen of England, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Alice de Hales aproximadamente 1318. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 4 de agosto de 1338, em Framlingham Castle, Suffolk, Inglaterra, com 38 anos, e foi sepultado em Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas of Brotherton 1st Earl of Norfolk родился 1 июня 1300 г. в местности бротиртон, еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Edward I King of England было 60 лет, а матери, которую звали Marguerite Capet de France Queen of England, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice de Hales около 1318 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Он умер 4 августа 1338 г. в местности фрамлингхам кастл, суффолк, Англия в возрасте 38 лет и был похоронен в местности Англия.
